summary: DeVault breaks down the confusing fee structures of donation platforms used to support creators. He categorizes fees into transaction fees, platform fees, withdrawal fees, and taxes, explaining how each type works. The post provides a calculator tool to help donors understand exactly how much of their contribution reaches the creator across different platforms. He compares fosspay (his own project, which only charges Stripe's transaction fee), Patreon (which layers on platform and withdrawal fees), and Liberapay.
key_points: Donation platform fees are opaque and confusing, consisting of multiple layers: transaction, platform, withdrawal, and taxes
fosspay, DeVault's own tool, has the lowest fees since it only passes through Stripe's transaction fee with no platform cut
Patreon adds both platform fees and withdrawal fees on top of payment processing, taking a larger share
Liberapay offers a lower-fee alternative to Patreon for creator support
money_quotes: Understanding fees are a really confusing part of supporting creators of things you like.
Transaction fees are charged by the payment processor (the company that takes down your card number and runs the transaction with your bank).
Platform fees are charged by the platform (e.g. Patreon) to run their operation, typically in the form of a fixed percentage of the transaction.
All of this adds up to a very confusing picture.
Only the typical Stripe fee is applied.
key_insight: Donation platforms obscure their true cost through layered fee structures, and simpler tools like fosspay that only pass through payment processor fees give creators a much better deal.
